title: How Loan Interest Works in Simple Terms
excerpt: Understand how interest affects your total repayment and monthly planning.
content: Loan interest is the cost of borrowing money. It is usually expressed as a yearly percentage rate.
Your monthly payment is made up of principal and interest. Early payments usually include more interest than principal.
Lowering your interest rate, even by one percentage point, can reduce the total amount you repay over the life of the loan.
Use a loan calculator to compare rates and terms before committing.

title: Choosing a Loan Term: Years vs Months
excerpt: Learn how shorter and longer terms change monthly costs and total interest.
content: A shorter term usually means higher monthly payments but lower total interest.
A longer term often feels more affordable monthly, but it can increase the total interest paid over time.
The right term depends on your cash flow, emergency savings, and long-term goals.
Run multiple scenarios in your calculator before making a decision.
